title: privilege_escalation_local
description: An endpoint detection system flags a standard user account exploiting an unpatched kernel vulnerability to gain SYSTEM-level privileges on a corporate workstation.

timeline.title: Suspicious Process Execution Detected
timeline.description: EDR alert fires: a standard user account is executing a known local privilege escalation exploit targeting an unpatched Windows kernel vulnerability.

timeline.title: SYSTEM Privileges Achieved
timeline.description: The exploit succeeds. The attacker now has SYSTEM-level access on the endpoint.

timeline.title: Persistence Mechanism Installed
timeline.description: A persistent backdoor is installed in a system directory and registered as a Windows service.

timeline.title: Internal Reconnaissance Begins
timeline.description: The attacker scans internal network ranges from the compromised endpoint, probing for accessible services.

timeline.title: Endpoint Quarantined
timeline.description: The workstation is isolated from the network. Credentials of the affected user are revoked.

timeline.title: Patch Deployed
timeline.description: The exploited vulnerability is patched across all affected systems. Least-privilege policies reviewed.

node-1.title: EDR Alert: Local Privilege Escalation Attempt
node-1.situation: An EDR alert fires on a user workstation. A standard user account is executing a known exploit for an unpatched Windows kernel vulnerability (CVE listed in the alert). The exploit is associated with local privilege escalation to SYSTEM.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Isolate the workstation from the network immediately and begin a forensic investigation.

options.label: B
options.text: Dismiss the alert as a false positive — standard users cannot normally gain elevated privileges.

options.label: C
options.text: Reboot the workstation to interrupt the exploit before investigating.

node-2.title: SYSTEM Privileges Confirmed
node-2.situation: Forensic review confirms the exploit succeeded before the workstation was isolated. The attacker now has SYSTEM-level privileges on the endpoint. A persistent backdoor service has been installed and network scanning activity was detected before isolation.
node-2.question: What do you do?

options.label: A
options.text: Revoke the affected user's credentials across all systems, preserve a forensic memory and disk image, and keep the workstation isolated pending full analysis.

options.label: B
options.text: Reboot the workstation to clear the exploit and return it to the user.

node-3.title: Backdoor and Reconnaissance Discovered
node-3.situation: Forensics confirms a persistent backdoor was installed and the attacker conducted internal network scanning before isolation. It is unclear whether any other systems were compromised during the scanning activity.
node-3.question: How do you contain the broader threat?

options.label: A
options.text: Scan all endpoints for indicators of compromise (IOCs) from the backdoor, revoke all active sessions for the affected user, and review network traffic logs for any lateral movement.

options.label: B
options.text: Re-image the workstation and consider the incident closed since the endpoint is isolated.

node-4.title: Patch Management Failure Identified
node-4.situation: Investigation reveals the exploited kernel vulnerability had an available patch that was not applied due to a gap in the patch management process. The vulnerability is present on 40 other endpoints. No lateral movement to other systems was confirmed.
node-4.question: How do you prevent recurrence?

options.label: A
options.text: Deploy the missing patch to all 40 affected endpoints immediately, enforce least-privilege user policies, and initiate a review of the patch management process to close coverage gaps.

options.label: B
options.text: Only patch the single workstation involved in the incident.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The compromised workstation has been re-imaged and the user's credentials rotated. The exploited vulnerability has been patched across all affected endpoints. Least-privilege policies are under review and the patch management process has been audited. No lateral movement was confirmed.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
